title: The best movies on Netflix right now (June 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's June 2026 list includes new originals and classic films like 'The Big Lebowski' and 'Creed'.
'Inside Man', starring Denzel Washington, tops the bank heist genre on Netflix's curated list.
Olivia Colman's 'The Lost Daughter', based on Elena Ferrante's novel, is among must-watch dramas on Netflix.

title: A guide to the New England ships in Sail Boston 2026
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will feature over 60 ships from 20 countries, celebrating the 250th anniversary of the United States.
The Parade of Sail, led by the USS Constitution, will take place in Boston Harbor on July 11.
Visitors can enjoy ship tours and events in Boston from July 11 to 16, as part of the Sail250 tour.

title: Large fire destroys multiple boats at Winthrop yacht club
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A large fire at Cottage Park Yacht Club in Winthrop destroyed at least 12 boats early Friday morning.
Firefighters and marine units from multiple areas, including Massport, responded to the blaze; no injuries were reported.
Some boats exploded and drifted away due to the fire, which made access difficult for marine units due to low tide.

title: Can Quincy chef Laurence Louie win ‘Top Chef’? Here’s what he said before the finale.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Quincy chef Laurence Louie advances to the 'Top Chef' finale after defeating Bobby Flay in a Food Network showdown.
Louie, owner of Rubato, highlights his journey from a history student to a reality TV cooking sensation.
Rubato's popularity grows as Louie's TV appearances draw attention to Quincy, enhancing its local food scene.

title: Needham Park and Rec chair charged with fraud, accused of stealing from Little League group
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Christopher Gerstel, Needham Park and Recreation Commission chair, charged with stealing over $250,000 from Little League organization.
Gerstel allegedly used the funds to pay personal debts, cover car payments, and finance country club and casino expenses.
He faces 17 charges, including wire fraud and tax-related offenses, with potential penalties up to 20 years in prison.

title: ‘Norway is coming’: Norwegian team releases Viking-themed photo ahead of World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Norway's men's soccer team will play in its first World Cup since 1998, starting with a match against Iraq on June 16.
Norway is considered a dark horse contender, led by stars Erling Haaland and Martin Ødegaard, with +3500 odds to win.
Norway released a Viking-themed promotional photo ahead of the World Cup, captioned 'Norway is coming.'

title: Boatslip Resort & Beach Club in Provincetown unveils new look
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Boatslip Resort & Beach Club in Provincetown underwent major renovations, updating guest rooms, hallways, and lobby spaces for the first time since 2009.
Designer David Thorn reimagined the Boatslip's interiors, incorporating natural colors and local artwork for a modernized, nautical-themed look.
The Boatslip expanded with the purchase of Liberty House, a historic nearby building, adding additional guest accommodations.

title: Man allegedly strikes two 99 Restaurant patrons with padlock on lanyard
author: John Waller
bullets: A man allegedly attacked two patrons at Charlestown's 99 Restaurant with a padlock on May 26.
Boston Police released a suspect photo and are seeking public assistance in identifying him.
The victims suffered head lacerations, requiring hospital treatment, and police continue to investigate.

title: Here’s how Newton’s 822-unit housing development finally happened
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Newton's Pattern District project includes 822 units, with 145 designated for lower-income tenants, surrounding 1.5 acres of green space.
The project, developed by Northland Investment Corp., faced a decade-long approval process, including a successful 2020 referendum.
Northland's development aims to be the largest privately funded Passive House project in the U.S., with $100 million invested so far.

title: The ultimate walk around Boston debuts this weekend, and it’s 91 miles long
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The 91-mile Boston Boundless Trail launches this Saturday, tracing a loop around Boston's perimeter.
The trail is divided into 12 sections, beginning in Boston’s North End and ending in East Boston.
Created by Walking City Trails, the trail uses existing paths and integrates public transit access.
